SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper describes a clear experimental setup (leave-one-out multi-source transfer on UD v1.2, use of Malt, MST and the UDP of Mareček & Straka) and reports comparative results (references to Table 5 and performance numbers versus McDonald et al. and Rosa). However, important details and evidence are missing in the provided text: the actual tables/metrics are not shown here, no statistical significance tests are reported, and some choices (e.g. truncating each language to the first 10,000 tokens claiming ~300k tokens is "enough") are asserted without empirical justification. There are also implicit assumptions (manual priors, setting many λattach=0) whose impact is not fully analyzed, leaving some gaps between claims and presented evidence.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The work applies existing approaches (multi-source delexicalized transfer as in McDonald et al., Malt/MST parsers, and the UDP of Mareček & Straka) to the Universal Dependencies collection and adds manually specified external priors for an unsupervised parser. This is a non-trivial application and evaluation across 32 languages, but the core methods and ideas (delexicalized transfer, using external priors) are largely existing, so the contribution appears incremental rather than a fundamentally new method.

CLARITY.justification: The paper generally describes datasets, preprocessing (delexicalization: replacing forms/lemmas/morphology/relations by underscores), and methods (leave-one-out multi-source transfer, manual priors pextstop/pextattach). However, the provided text contains repeated and overlapping paragraphs, some formatting artifacts, and references to tables (Tables 1–5, 3, 4) that are not shown here, which hampers readability and makes it harder to follow the full experimental story and reproduce exact rule settings.

SUBSTANCE.justification: The study evaluates methods on a reasonably large set (32 languages) and compares to prior works, and explores both manually assigned and learned priors. Yet the paper lacks detailed ablations (e.g. sensitivity to the 10k-token trimming, quantitative effect of each prior rule, and statistical validation), and limitations/failure cases are only briefly hinted (e.g. better performance on non-Indo-European languages) without deeper analysis, reducing the overall experimental depth.

IMPACT.justification: Addressing cross-lingual transfer and minimal supervision for dependency parsing on the Universal Dependencies resource is an important and practical problem, and the finding that a minimally supervised parser helps non-Indo-European languages is potentially useful. Nevertheless, the contribution seems evolutionary rather than transformative and is mainly of moderate impact to researchers working on low-resource parsing and UD-based transfer.

APPROPRIATENESS.justification: The topic (cross-lingual delexicalized transfer and minimally supervised dependency parsing evaluated on Universal Dependencies) clearly falls within the scope of top-tier NLP/ML venues. The audience and evaluation style (parser comparisons, multi-language experiments) match community expectations and standards for such conferences.

MEANINGFUL_COMPARISON.justification: The paper compares its transfer parser results to prior work (McDonald et al. 2011 and Rosa 2015) and reports higher averaged accuracies (e.g. 70.1% vs 61.7% and 62.5% vs 56.6% on shared languages), arguing UD harmonization helps transfer. The text also notes comparability caveats (different annotation styles), but full fairness details (identical data splits, metrics, and statistical tests) are not fully presented in the excerpt.

REPLICABILITY.justification: The paper gives several reproducibility details: UD v1.2, use of first treebank per language (32 languages), explicit delexicalization procedure, use of Malt, MST and UDP implementations, and that first 10,000 tokens per language were used. However, critical specifics are missing in the provided text (exact manual prior values are referenced but contained in unavailable Tables 3 and 4, some hyperparameters and λ values are only qualitatively described, and modified parser code is mentioned without release), so full reproduction from this text alone would be difficult.
